generated_oml:

]

    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraStereoVision

    base:desc "Measures servo position for steering mechanism"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance LiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP"
]

instance CameraStereoVision : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
    base:desc "Stereo vision data streams from Intel Deepsense D435i camera"
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [

    base:desc "Vehicle state information including speed and position"
]

instance MotorSpeedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSpeedSensor
    base:desc "Motor speed data for propulsion control"
]

    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
    base:desc "Steering angle data for vehicle direction"
]

instance ServoPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoPositionSensor
    base:desc "Servo position data for steering mechanism"
]

instance LiDARProcessedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringEnabler
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR point cloud data with aligned message types and unit conversions"
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Ego vehicle state information stored in SQL database"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabaseEnabler
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ObjectTrackingEnabler

]

    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksData

]

    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter for object tracking"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware for integrating application modules using ROS2"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance NVIDIAJetpack5_0 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software Development Kit (SDK) for NVIDIA Jetson platform"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance Python3_8 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Programming language for application modules"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

    base:desc "Programming language for high-performance modules"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tool for model-based design and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance PointCloudClusteringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ObjectTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component for tracking objects using Kalman filtering"

]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for persistent storage of object tracks and vehicle state"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ACCComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface for real-time visualization of vehicle state data, projected driving paths, and detected object tracks, alongside data saving and report generation"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateVisualization, ProjectedPathVisualization, ObjectTracksVisualization, DataSaving, ReportGeneration
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Processing of ACC system's lead vehicle detection, speed calculation, and command generation externally to alleviate on-board ECU constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetection, SpeedCalculation, CommandGeneration
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote enable/disable ACC, set target speeds, issue emergency braking commands, and developer troubleshooting via data collection and monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteEnableDisableACC, SetTargetSpeed, EmergencyBraking, DataCollectionForTroubleshooting
]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed into motor voltage commands for vehicle propulsion"
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls vehicle steering through precise servo mechanisms"
]

instance PhysicalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical Twin environment containing all physical components of the 1/10-scale vehicle"
    base:contains VedderElectronicSpeedController, SteeringServo, HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor, MotorSpeedSensor, SteeringAngleSensor, ServoPositionSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin environment handling data processing and computation offloading"
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringEnabler, ObjectTrackingEnabler, ACCComputationEnabler, SQLDatabaseEnabler, ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetpack5_0, Python3_8, CPlusPlus, MATLABSimulink
]

instance UserEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User environment for visualization and control"
    base:contains DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands generated by the Controlling FE are serialized by the Data Translation component and transmitted over Wi-Fi UDP to the Actuation FE for execution on physical actuators. The Data Translation component handles serialization, message type alignment, and unit conversions for command data streams."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system requires 100 ms clock synchronization accuracy for safety-critical vehicular applications due to hardware limitations, ensuring coordination between devices for computation offloading."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The architecture follows the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture with modifications, deploying each functional entity (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User) on distinct hardware. Components are organized into ROS2 packages where each FE is implemented as a ROS2 node, facilitating system-wide coordination through ROS2 middleware."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The system enforces real-time and high-fidelity computational reflections, with validation requirements including implementation of Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) per ISO 15622. Key fidelity constraints include maximum network latency of 100 ms (Requirement R7) and clock synchronization accuracy of 100 ms (Requirement R8) across all components."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Internal communication between software components uses ROS2, while inter-entity communication occurs over Wi-Fi using UDP in the transport layer. Requirement R7 specifies a maximum network latency of 100 ms. Bandwidth requirements and redundancy mechanisms are not detailed in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Adherence to ISO 23247:2021, which specifies a four-part framework: Part 1 (Overview and general principles), Part 2 (Reference architecture), Part 3 (Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements), and Part 4 (Information exchange). The reference architecture (Part 2) employs an entity-based model where the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) represents physical entities (e.g., equipment, facilities, products), the Data Collection and Device Control Entity handles data acquisition and control of OMEs, the Digital Twin Entity provides a digital representation of OMEs, and the User Entity offers interfaces for interaction (e.g., monitoring data, control). Each entity comprises Functional Entities (FEs) for specific tasks like actuation, data transformation, and user interfacing. The standard does not dictate FEs' implementation, with OPC Unified Architecture cited as a possible protocol."
]
